key_points: Algebraic expressions are formed using variables, constants and operations
A term is a single part of an algebraic expression
Like terms have the same variables with the same powers
Unlike terms have different variables or powers
The distributive property is used to expand brackets in algebra
Multiplication of expressions follows the distributive rule
Expressions can be simplified by combining like terms
exam_tips: Identify like terms before simplifying expressions
Use distributive property carefully while expanding brackets
Pay attention to signs when multiplying expressions
Write steps clearly to avoid algebra mistakes
